description:
PACT v0.3 RISC Zero Guest Program — Input Schema
Generated by: NotBob (Agent of Bob Lyons)
Purpose: Documents the exact input format the PACT host must provide to the RISC Zero prover.

Two-part input: public inputs (verifier sees) + private witness (prover only, circuit-private).

ARCHITECTURE NOTE:
  The circuit proves two things simultaneously:
    1. policy_hash is anchored at log_index in the transparency log (Merkle proof)
    2. tool_name is in policy.allowed_tools (membership proof)

  Private data (allowed_tools, full policy) never leaves the guest VM.
  The verifier only sees public inputs + proof bytes.

public.description: Public inputs — visible to the verifier after proof generation

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the committed policy document (hex, with sha256: prefix)

merkle_root.description: Merkle root from the transparency log entry at log_index

log_index.description: Index of the log entry in the transparency log (0-indexed)

tool_name_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the tool name being called (hex, with sha256: prefix)

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the tool call (UTC)

params_hash.description: Optional: SHA-256 hash of tool parameters (hex, with sha256: prefix). If absent, defaults to sha256:empty.

private.description: Private witness — known only to the prover, circuit-private (never sent to verifier)

allowed_tools.description: Full list of tool names in the policy's allowed_tools set

merkle_proof.description: Merkle inclusion proof: path from policy_hash leaf to merkle_root

hash.description: Sibling hash at this level (hex, sha256: prefix)

is_right.description: true = sibling is right child (node || sibling); false = sibling is left child (sibling || node)

log_id.description: Full log_id from the transparency log entry (sha256:...)
